title: Your Nimclea Structural Diagnostic
subtitle: Based on your responses, your system shows patterns commonly seen in organizations under structural pressure.

summary: Your responses suggest that supporting evidence and final outputs may be somewhat harder to locate or align than they should be.
These signals appear early, but they are worth addressing before verification pressure increases.
Organizations in this pattern typically benefit from clarifying how evidence, retrieval, and version control interact.
Your responses suggest that supporting evidence and final outputs may be harder to locate, align, or reconstruct than they should be.

These patterns point to active traceability risk, not just early warning signs.

evidence_fragmentation_score.label: Evidence Fragmentation
evidence_fragmentation_score.description: Supporting records appear to be distributed across multiple locations, formats, or retrieval paths, which can slow verification and increase reconstruction effort.

governance_strength_score.label: Responsibility & Approval Weakness
governance_strength_score.description: Approval ownership, review discipline, or change accountability may not always be explicit enough to keep reporting and operational changes easy to explain.

complexity_score.label: Cross-Team / Cross-System Drift
complexity_score.description: Multiple teams, definitions, or systems may be contributing to drift in how final results are assembled or interpreted.

pressure_context_score.label: Pressure-Revealed Risk
pressure_context_score.description: The most serious structural weaknesses may become visible only when pressure increases, such as during audit requests, deadlines, or incident handling.

summary: Your responses suggest emerging structural pressure in how evidence, approvals, or cross-team coordination are handled.
Some part of the current operating structure may be creating avoidable effort, delay, or uncertainty when results need to be explained or verified.
Nimclea can help make those pathways more visible and easier to verify.

top_signals.label: Structural Friction
top_signals.description: Some part of the current operating structure may be creating avoidable effort, delay, or uncertainty when results need to be explained or verified.
